title: mammillaria elegans var. dealbata
intro: Mammillaria elegans var. dealbata, also known as the hairy pincushion cactus or white-flowered pincushion cactus, is a rare and unique variety of the Mammillaria elegans species, characterized by its white, woolly spines and delicate white flowers. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
